attributes.stem: A 74-year-old woman presents with a lump in her lower abdominal region. This has been present for 'many years' and has not bothered until recently when it started to get bigger and is more evident when she coughs. She does not notice it when she lies down. Her medical history includes type 2 diabetes and hypertension. She takes metformin and perindopril. She has a 30 pack-year smoking history.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most most likely diagnosis?
options: Hydrocele of the canal of Nuck
attributes.explanation:
This woman has a right inguinal hernia. She can push it back when she wants to (Image).

Inguinal hernias are the most common type of groin hernia and outnumber femoral hernias by 15 to 1. Femoral hernias occur more frequently in women, but still the most common type of groin hernia in women is the inguinal hernia. Inguinal hernias may either be direct or indirect. An indirect hernia is a protrusion (typically fat and/or intestine) through the deep inguinal ring and into the inguinal canal. A direct hernia is a direct protrusion through a (weakened) posterior wall of the inguinal canal. For practical purposes, there is little clinical significance in this distinction.

Femoral hernias are a protrusion through the femoral canal and because of the canal's relative rigidity, reduction of a femoral hernia is not easy. In this case the groin swelling disappears when the woman lies down, so from that information alone, it is very unlikely to be a femoral hernia.

A Spigelian hernia represents a weak point at the lateral border of the rectus abdominal sheath (linea semilunaris) where it is attached to the internal oblique muscle. The defect is usually below the arcuate line, where there is no posterior rectus sheath. The swelling shown in this patient is below and medial to where a Spigelian hernia might be.

Whilst a lipoma might occur at this site, it would not disappear when the the woman lies down.

The canal of Nuck is a remnant of parietal peritoneum extending from the round ligament into the inguinal canal. Very rarely it can be associated with an indirect inguinal hernia or a walled-off collection of fluid - the so-called hydrocele of the canal of Nuck.
learningPoints: Although femoral hernias occur more frequently in woman, the common type of hernia seen in women is still the inguinal hernia.
Inguinal Hernias are the most common groin hernia to undergo repair (97% of all hernias).
references: Itani KMF, Fitzgibbons R. Approach to Groin Hernias. JAMA Surg 2019; 154:551-52.
